subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Does not divide by 100 — uses rate as a whole number multiplier
explanation: Student uses R directly in PRT instead of R/100, producing answers 100× too large.
why_students_think_this: Knows the formula has R but doesn't internalize that R% means R/100. Percentage symbol isn't operationalized.
grade8_impact: All percentage and interest calculations will be 100× wrong. Affects every finance topic.
grade12_impact: Will make systematic errors in compound interest, depreciation, and financial mathematics.
root_cause: Does not understand that % means 'per hundred' — percent symbol not converted to decimal

subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Ignores time — computes SI for 1 year only
explanation: Calculates (P × R) / 100 and stops, treating T = 1 implicitly even when T > 1.
why_students_think_this: Has a partial formula that doesn't include time, or treats T = 1 as the default case.
grade8_impact: Systematically underestimates interest. Errors in all multi-year problems.
grade12_impact: Cannot correctly compute loan repayments or investment returns over time.
root_cause: Incomplete formula internalization — T is not part of the mental formula

subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Confuses or misremembers the SI formula
explanation: Uses an incorrect variant of SI = PRT/100, such as mixing up which variable goes where or using the wrong denominator.
why_students_think_this: Memorized the formula without understanding what P, R, and T represent. Gets confused when rearranging.
grade8_impact: Unpredictable errors across all SI problem types.
grade12_impact: Similar formula confusion will appear in physics (v = u + at), chemistry, and financial math.
root_cause: Formula memorization without conceptual understanding of what each component means

subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Confuses Simple Interest with Total Amount
explanation: Treats the Amount (P + SI) as the interest earned, or vice versa — uses Amount where SI is needed in the formula.
why_students_think_this: Both terms appear in word problems. 'Gets back ₹X' is confused with 'earns ₹X as interest'.
grade8_impact: Produces answers that are too high or too low by the principal amount. Very common error.
grade12_impact: Errors in compound interest, loan EMI, and all financial calculations involving principal + interest.
root_cause: Lacks the part-whole distinction between interest earned and total money returned

subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Uses months directly as years without converting
explanation: Substitutes T = 18 months as T = 18 in the formula instead of T = 18/12 = 1.5 years.
why_students_think_this: Time is given numerically and the student plugs it in directly without noticing the unit. Rate is per annum so T must be in years.
grade8_impact: Major calculation errors when time is given in months or days. Very common in exam questions.
grade12_impact: Will make unit errors in physics kinematics, chemistry half-life, and financial math.
root_cause: Does not check units before substituting into formulas

subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Uses plain percentage formula instead of SI formula when finding rate
explanation: Computes Rate = (SI/P) × 100 ignoring time T. This gives annual rate × T, not the annual rate.
why_students_think_this: The simple percentage formula (part/whole × 100) is more familiar and gets applied by default when finding a percentage rate.
grade8_impact: Rate found will be T times too large. Systematic errors in all 'find the rate' problems.
grade12_impact: Cannot correctly compute rates of return, growth rates, or percentage change in context.
root_cause: Defaults to percentage formula without recognizing that time is a factor in interest rate

subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Incorrectly rearranges SI formula to find P, R, or T
explanation: Knows SI = PRT/100 but makes algebraic errors when isolating a different variable.
why_students_think_this: Has not practised formula rearrangement. Tries to substitute and solve rather than isolating the unknown algebraically.
grade8_impact: Errors on any problem that requires finding P, R, or T given the other quantities.
grade12_impact: Algebraic manipulation skills are foundational for all higher mathematics.
root_cause: Weak algebraic manipulation skills — cannot isolate a variable from a multi-term formula

subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Applies compound interest logic to simple interest problems
explanation: Adds interest to principal at the end of each year and computes interest on the new total — treating SI as CI.
why_students_think_this: May have encountered compound interest informally (bank apps, savings accounts) and defaults to this more complex model.
grade8_impact: Produces answers slightly above the correct SI. Conceptual confusion about interest models.
grade12_impact: Must understand both models clearly for Commerce and Science streams in Grade 11-12.
root_cause: Real-world experience with compound interest banks without formal understanding of the distinction

subject: simple_interest
misconception_name: Uses combined principal with a single rate for multi-loan problems
explanation: When multiple amounts are borrowed at different rates, adds all principals together and uses one rate instead of computing SI separately for each.
why_students_think_this: Correct procedure for single-rate problems, over-generalised to multi-rate contexts.
grade8_impact: Errors on all problems involving multiple investment/loan scenarios.
grade12_impact: Portfolio calculations, blended cost of capital — foundational for Commerce students.
root_cause: Over-generalisation of single-principal procedure to multi-principal contexts
